title: Number of parties in multilateral environmental agreements
subtitle: Total number of global parties signed on to multilateral agreements designed to address trans-boundary environmental issues.
caption:
Number of parties in multilateral environmental agreements
Total number of global parties signed on to multilateral agreements designed to address trans-boundary environmental issues.

The line chart tracks the cumulative number of global parties joining various multilateral environmental agreements between 1971 and 2015. The vertical axis measures the count of parties from 0 to 200, while the horizontal axis spans the years. The lines show a consistent upward trajectory for all listed treaties, indicating that participation in these agreements has grown over time. The Vienna Convention on the Ozone Layer and the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) display the highest participation levels, with their lines reaching the top of the chart near 200 parties by 2015. The Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD) and the UN Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD) also show high participation, with lines ending just below the 200 mark. The World Heritage Convention and the Kyoto Protocol follow a similar path, with lines rising to approximately 190 parties. In the middle of the range, the Cites Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora and the Stockholm Convention on Persistent Organic Pollutants show lines ending between 150 and 180 parties. The Ramsar Convention (Wetlands) and the Cartagena Protocol on Biosafety show lines reaching roughly 160 to 170 parties. The UN Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS) and the Rotterdam Convention (Hazardous chemicals) show lines ending around 150 to 160 parties. The Convention on the Conservation of Migratory Species of Wild Animals shows the lowest participation among the group, with its line ending near 120 parties. The data shows that the number of parties in multilateral environmental agreements has increased for all listed treaties from 1971 to 2015.
